exemplar.title: To what extent does a coach's behavior have an impact on an athlete's motivation towards their sport?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does a coach's behavior have an impact on an athlete's motivation towards their sport?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Topic and purpose are communicated accurately and effectively from the introduction through recurring signposting.
Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to all analytical chapters.
Methodology chapter outlines a complete range of relevant methods with clear evidence of selection.
weaknesses: Minor imprecision in phrasing (e.g., âdetrimentalâ vs âcrucialâ) slightly affects clarity.
Justification for inclusion/exclusion criteria in methodology could be more explicit.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently communicates the focus and purpose with clarity and maintains a strong link to the research question throughout. Although there are minor lapses in phrasing and methodological justification, the work fully meets the descriptors for the top band. Hence a full 6 is warranted.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of key sport-psychology terminology (e.g., intrinsic/extrinsic motivation, autonomy-supportive).
Coherent integration of multiple theoretical perspectives (SDT, self-efficacy) across chapters.
Source material is generally relevant and applied meaningfully to the research focus.
weaknesses: Occasional mis-wording (e.g., âdetrimentalâ) and factual inconsistencies in citations.
Some sources are described rather than critically analyzed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While a few minor inaccuracies appear, the essay demonstrates excellent subject knowledge, coherent understanding and strong application of terminology. This performance aligns with the upper range of the 5â6 band, meriting a score of 5.

strengths: Research draws on a well-chosen set of empirical studies directly relevant to the research question.
Analysis is clear with supported conclusions linking back to theory and empirical findings.
The discussion chapter acknowledges methodological limitations and ethical concerns, showing critical evaluation.
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive at points, with limited discussion of effect sizes or statistical significance.
Evaluation of contrasting evidence (e.g., autocratic vs autonomy-supportive coaching) could be more deeply integrated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits good-to-excellent research relevance, clear analysis and partially critical evaluation, aligning with the 7â9 band descriptors. While deeper statistical scrutiny and more sustained critical synthesis would push it higher, a score of 9 accurately reflects current performance.

strengths: Structure fully meets IB conventions with clear chapter headings and logical flow.
Table of contents and page numbering aid navigation.
Layout is generally consistent and professional.
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation style (mix of Harvard and URLs) and occasional typographic artifacts.
A few sentence fragments and missing text in the methods section disrupt readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is strong overall, with a clearly appropriate structure and mostly correct formatting. Minor layout and typographic issues are present but do not impede comprehension, justifying the top band score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Terminology such as âintrinsic/extrinsic motivation,â âautonomy-supportive,â âego-oriented climate,â and âcortisolâ is used appropriately and consistently.  Occasional mis-wording (âdetrimentalâ for âcriticalâ; âself-constructive evaluation toolâ) shows minor imprecision, so the performance is âmostly accurateâ rather than flawless, justifying a score of 4 within the 3-4 band.

Word count, title and page numbers are provided, and basic formatting is consistent.  However, citation style is uneven (mix of Harvard-style and raw URLs), and some typographic elements (e.g., extra spaces, LaTeX-style symbols) detract from professional presentation.  Overall layout considerations are present but not flawlessly applied, fitting the lower end of the 3-4 band with a score of 3.

The essay summarises core theories (Self-Determination Theory, self-efficacy) accurately and applies them to the chosen studies; cortisol, motivational climate and psychoneuroendocrine concepts are explained with reasonable correctness.  Source material is generally relevant and integrated, though some citations (e.g., Donald et al., 2022) are used descriptively rather than analytically and a few factual inaccuracies appear.  Overall knowledge is good but not consistently excellent, placing the work in the 3-4 band at 4.

A discrete âMethodologyâ chapter identifies observational, survey and interview studies and comments on strengths, weaknesses and potential bias; later chapters revisit methodological limitations.  The range of peer-reviewed sources is reasonable, but selection leans heavily on a handful of secondary reviews and there is limited justification for inclusion/exclusion criteria.  These features align with "mostly complete methodologyâ¦ some evidence of informed selection," meriting the top of the 3-4 band (4).

The student clearly communicates the topic of coach behaviour and athlete motivation from the outset, provides an explicit definition of key constructs (intrinsic / extrinsic motivation) and situates the issue within sport-psychology theory.  The introduction, chapter headings and recurring reminders of purpose maintain a consistent focus on the guiding idea throughout the essay.  Minor lapses in phrasing (âdetrimentalâ instead of âcrucialâ) do not substantially reduce clarity, so the quality best fits the upper band but with some small imprecision, warranting 5 rather than 6.

The student draws on a selection of empirical studies (Manule et al., Castillo & Espinosa, Bartholomew et al., Smith et al.) that are directly tied to coaching style and motivation, showing clear relevance.  However, citation breadth is moderate and heavily weighted toward English-language secondary literature, so research quality is âgoodâ rather than âexcellent,â sitting comfortably at 5 in the 4-6 band.

The research question â âTo what extent does a coachâs behavior have an impact on an athleteâs motivation towards their sport?â â is stated verbatim, is narrow enough for a 4 000-word essay, and is returned to in each analytical chapter and in the conclusion.  The wording is unambiguous and the discussion consistently links findings back to this focus.  Because the connection is sustained but the conceptual depth of the question (e.g., specifying type of motivation or population) could be sharper, a solid 5 is appropriate.

The essay follows IB conventions: title page, table of contents, numbered chapters, introductionâmethodologyâdiscussionâconclusion, and bibliography.  Headings are clear and logical, facilitating reader navigation; no major elements are missing.  Minor issues (inconsistent sub-heading spacing) prevent a perfect score, so 4 in the 3-4 band is appropriate.

The discussion chapter recognises sample-size issues, self-report bias and cross-sectional limitations, showing partial critical evaluation.  Arguments are coherent, yet weighing of contrasting evidence (e.g., benefits of autocratic style for novices) is brief and not deeply integrated into the overall conclusion.  Hence, the strand aligns with âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluation,â earning 4 within the 4-6 band.

Chapters consistently compare findings, note methodological limits and relate evidence to SDT; nevertheless, many statements remain descriptive (e.g., repeating study results) with limited synthesis or statistical scrutiny.  Conclusions are usually supported but occasionally overstated, indicating clear but not fully developed analysis.  This matches the mid-level of the 4-6 band (score 4).

annotations.comment: In âChapter 7 : Discussionâ, the extra space before the colon is inconsistent with IB formatting conventions.
The introduction effectively establishes the significance of coaching behaviors in sports performance, engaging the reader.
The inclusion of a clear word count (âWord Count: 3870â) demonstrates adherence to the IB formatting requirements.
The research question is prominently stated at the start, maintaining clear focus on coach behavior and athlete motivation.
The title âTopic: Sport psychologyâ is overly generic. Consider refining it to specifically reference coach behavior and motivation.
The table of contents on this page is comprehensive and clearly structured, aiding navigation.
The term âdetrimentalâ in âself-efficacyâ¦ belief is detrimentalâ is misused; consider âinstrumentalâ or âfoundationalâ instead.
The discussion acknowledges key methodological limitations and ethical concerns, illustrating critical evaluation.
The sentence fragment âA better approach would be longitudinalâ ends abruptly; complete the thought.
Fragmented continuation on page 17 should be merged with the preceding line for coherence.
The justification of Bartholomew et al. (2009) in the context of SDT demonstrates focused research selection.
The conclusion provides a concise summary linking back to the research question and theory.
Citation âDonald, Marvin, Farmer and Cypress (2022)â is non-standard. Use âDonald et al. (2022)â or Harvard style consistently.
Definitions of intrinsic and extrinsic motivation are clear; this demonstrates good use of key sport-psychology concepts.
This paragraph describes definitions but does not yet tie them explicitly back to the research question; consider signposting that link.
The linkage between SDT and coaching in this section demonstrates solid theoretical grounding.
The aim statement in the second paragraph is verbose and repeats prior wording; condense for clarity and directness.
Methodology overview names multiple methods but lacks clear justification for why these were selected.
Consider adding explicit inclusion and exclusion criteria to strengthen the rationale for source selection.
Description of observational, survey, and interview methods effectively notes both benefits and limitations.
In the Castillo and Espinosa citation, the in-text year (2017) contradicts the bibliography year (2014). Verify accuracy.
Inclusion of Banduraâs self-efficacy theory enriches the framework and links theory to empirical studies.
The citation âBandura (1997)â conflicts with the bibliography entry dated 2014; cross-check publication details.
Chapter headings are clearly formatted and improve the structure of the extended essay.
The parenthetical explanation of psychoneuroendocrine responses is convoluted; simplify for readability.
The methods section cuts off mid-sentence (âThey completed aâ), indicating missing text.
Including the 24- and 14-question survey details illustrates a thorough approach to data collection.
The inline LaTeX fragment <math display="inline">(2016)</math> is not rendered correctly; remove MathML or convert to plain text.
The section summarizing conflicting findings between studies demonstrates critical engagement with the literature.
The transition into SDT in Chapter 4 is clear, reinforcing theoretical application.
Analysis remains largely descriptive without commenting on effect sizes or statistical significance; deepen analytical rigor.
Explanation of intrinsicâextrinsic motivation spectrum is thorough and accurate.
When noting limitations of Bartholomew et al., consider specifying sample demographics to contextualize generalizability.
Strong linkage back to self-efficacy research highlights good integration of multiple theoretical perspectives.
When reporting the 20% and â7% changes, include confidence intervals or p-values to improve statistical transparency.
Referencing Banduraâs findings alongside Smith et al. shows thoughtful synthesis of sources.
Statistics from the Gervis and Dunn interviews are omitted; include frequency or thematic counts to support claims.
The description of lasting emotional damage demonstrates nuanced understanding of coaching impacts.
Page numbering is present and consistent throughout, supporting easy navigation.
Works cited entries display inconsistent URL formatting and spaces in links; apply uniform citation style.
Several entries mix raw URLs with Harvard formatting; standardize the bibliography according to one consistent style.
